title: Gerascanthus albus
taxonomy: Gerascanthus albus is classified as a species within the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleanders. The genus Gerascanthus is a part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which contains plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Further classification of Gerascanthus albus places it in the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that includes other families such as Loganiaceae and Gentianaceae. Members of the family Apocynaceae are typically characterized by their showy flowers and often toxic compounds. Within the genus Gerascanthus, Gerascanthus albus is one of several species that are native to specific regions.
